data.title: Google and OpenAI Advocate for Relaxed AI Training Regulations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Google and OpenAI have urged the U.S. government, led by President Donald Trump, to ease restrictions on AI training, particularly concerning the use of copyrighted materials. The tech giants submitted policy proposals on Thursday, advocating for a more flexible approach to artificial intelligence regulations as the U.S. government plans to develop its 'AI Action Plan' by mid-2025.

The proposals from Google and OpenAI respond to President Trump's recent focus on AI regulation, following his revocation of the previous administration's AI executive order in January 2025. Both companies argue that relaxing intellectual property barriers is crucial for fostering innovation and maintaining the United States' leadership in AI development.

Google emphasized that 'fair use and text and data mining exceptions' are vital for ongoing AI research, warning that stringent copyright policies could hinder advancements in areas such as healthcare, scientific discovery, and economic development.

data.title: IntoTheBlock: Bitcoin fees fell again this week and hit their lowest level since September last year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to IntoTheBlock data, Bitcoin fees fell again this week, hitting the lowest point since September 2024. Data showed that when the price of Bitcoin fell to a low of $80,780, the total network fee was about $2.5 million.

data.title: OKX Completes 27th OKB Destruction, Total OKB Destruction: 31,158,862
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, OKX released the OKB destruction announcement (December 1, 2024-February 28, 2025), and OKB completed the 27th destruction. The number of OKB destroyed is 31,158,862, and the total number of OKB destroyed is 171,305,654.15.

data.title: SEC Reportedly Evaluating XRP as a Commodity – Major Implications Ahead
data.description:
TL;DR The SEC is reconsidering XRP’s classification, assessing whether it should be treated as a commodity rather than a financial security. The lawsuit against Ripple Labs is in its final stages, with the company arguing that the evolving regulatory stance should influence penalty reductions. Reclassifying XRP as a commodity could provide greater regulatory clarity for ... 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/sec-reportedly-evaluating-xrp-as-a-commodity-major-implications-ahead/

data.title: WeChat reminder: Some criminals are committing fraud by falsely claiming to provide services such as stock recommendations and virtual currency trading
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: WeChat Security Center issued an announcement stating that it has recently received user complaints that some criminals have induced users to jump to third-party platforms in the name of "high-yield financial management", "guaranteed profits", "insider information", etc., and falsely claimed to provide stock recommendations, virtual currency transactions, foreign exchange investment and other services, but in fact they have committed fraud by falsifying data, manipulating price fluctuations, closing platforms and other means. The WeChat team will continue to strengthen the crackdown on false investment and financial fraud. For the above-mentioned illegal acts, after verification and confirmation, the relevant accounts will be subject to tiered penalties according to the severity of the violation. The WeChat Security Center reminds that investment is risky, and any investment project claiming "capital protection and high returns" or "low-risk and huge profits" is suspected of fraud. Please do not trust financial recommendations from informal channels, and do not transfer money to unfamiliar accounts or disclose personal financial information. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin mining company Cango announces $30 million share buyback program
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Cango, a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, announced that its board of directors has approved a stock repurchase plan to repurchase issued American Depositary Shares (ADS) and Class A common shares in the next 12 months starting from April 25, 2025, with a repurchase scale of US$30 million. According to Cango's previously disclosed operating data, the company's total Bitcoin holdings reached 1,944 as of the end of February. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Gold ETF Inflows Surge While Bitcoin ETF Experiences Outflows
data.description: According to Odaily, recent data reveals that over the past 30 days, gold ETFs have seen an inflow of $10 billion, whereas Bitcoin ETFs have experienced an outflow of $5 billion. Charlie Morris, founder of ByTree and manager of the BOLD ETF, believes that this trend will eventually reverse, as has been the case historically.

data.title: The “graduation rate” of the Meme coin on the Pump.fun platform has been below 1% for four consecutive weeks, hitting a record low
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Pump.fun’s token “graduation rate” has been below 1% for four consecutive weeks. On the platform, the “graduation rate” refers to the percentage of tokens that transition from the incubation stage to a fully tradable state on the Solana decentralized exchange (DEX). To qualify for graduation, a token must meet certain liquidity and trading requirements.
Dune Analytics data shows that since February 17, the "graduation rate" of the Pump.fun platform has been below 1% for four consecutive weeks for the first time. Previously, the platform’s graduation rate has been low, with the best performance in history occurring in November 2023, when 1.67% of tokens successfully entered the open market. At that time, Pump.fun created 323,000 tokens in the previous week, which means that about 5,400 tokens entered the Solana DeFi ecosystem.
However, as the popularity of Meme coins cooled, the number of tokens created in the Pump.fun and Solana ecosystems dropped significantly. Dune data shows that as of now, the number of tokens graduating from the platform each week has dropped to about 1,500, a significant decline from its peak.

data.title: BTC ETFs have seen $5 billion outflows in the past 30 days, while gold ETFs have seen inflows of around $10 billion, but the trend may be reversed
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Data shows that the inflow of funds into gold ETFs in the past 30 days was $10 billion, while the outflow of funds from Bitcoin ETFs was $5 billion. However, Charlie Morris, founder of ByTree and manager of BOLD ETF, believes that this flow of funds will reverse sooner or later, as usual. (Coindesk)
